(2) The prescribed officer shall enter particulars of the variation, revocation or recall in the register and give notice of the entry to—


(3) An application under section 28(2) of the 1986 Act must be made in accordance with the Part 19 procedure.


(4) The applicant for the Part 1 order, if not the applicant under section 28(2) of the 1986 Act, must be made a defendant to the application.


(5) Where the court cancels a registration under section 28(2) of the 1986 Act, the court officer will amend the register and give notice of the amendment to the court which made the Part 1 order.


### Interim directions E+W


32.29. The following persons will be made parties to an application for interim directions under section 29 of the 1986 Act M6 —


Marginal Citations


M6 Section 29 was amended by section 108(5) of and paragraphs 62(1) and (2)(a) of Schedule 13 to the Children Act 1989 and by section 15(1) of and paragraphs 2 and 4 of Schedule 2 to the Children and Adoption Act 2006.


### Staying and dismissal of enforcement proceedings E+W


32.30. —(1) The following persons will be made parties to an application under section 30(1) or 31(1) of the 1986 Act—


(2) Where the court makes an order under section 30(2) or (3) or section 31(3) of the 1986 Act, the court officer will amend the register and give notice of the amendment to—


### Particulars of other proceedings E+W


32.31. A party to proceedings for or relating to a Part 1 order who knows of other proceedings which relate to the child concerned (including proceedings out of the jurisdiction and concluded proceedings) must file a witness statement which—


### Inspection of register E+W


32.32. The following persons may inspect any entry in the register relating to a Part 1 order and may request copies of the order any document relating to it—
